verdict.label: Probably skip
verdict.headline: No reliable window
verdict.reason: Surfline and Surf-Forecast agree that the useful windows are poor.

conversationalSummary.text: TODAY there’s probably no reliable window—everything’s feeling too soft, and Surfline looks poor with the spot surf likely below your bodyboard fun size. Wind is only mixed, but the swell just isn’t really there, and Cerro won’t be much of a payoff even after work. I’d check Surfline and MEO Beachcam first and then save the drive unless the cams look noticeably better than expected.

best.bestWind: ideal SSW, usable S/SW/SSE/SE, clean <=22 km/h

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Cerro looks like a small window for Too soft, Low confidence: 0-0.3 m, energy not captured, 9 s period, lully 0/100, and wind is not fully clear.  A sheltered beachbreak with gentle lefts and rights, somewhat similar to Cantinho but less consistent; this is best for manageable bodyboard waves when north-northwest swell reaches the bay with clean southerly wind; 6-9s at mid tide can make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia sharp and rippable.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; Cerro looks below your bodyboard fun size.
best.shortExplanation: Cerro is worth a quick check for after work (18:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  The wind should be manageable, but not a big advantage.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.
warnings: Cerro looks below your bodyboard fun size
